variants: Pick a business name. Write 5 options that fit your niche.
Name your store. Come up with 5 catchy options for your brand.
Choose a store name. List 5 short, memorable names for your shop.
tasks.why: Day 1 aha moment — instant business name options from library

variants: Research demand. Search one idea on Amazon right now.
Research demand. Google one idea and see what comes up.
Research demand. Look up one idea on TikTok Shop.
tasks.why: Seeing what already sells tells you if there's demand.

variants: Pick one idea. Write which felt most interesting and why.
Pick one idea. Circle your favorite and write one reason.
Pick one idea. Write the one you'd most want to talk about.
tasks.why: You need one direction before you can move forward.

variants: Get a logo. Sketch 3 ideas that match your brand feel.
Create your logo. Pick a style and colors that fit your store.
Design a logo. Choose a look that represents your brand.
tasks.why: Day 2 aha moment — logo from template library using their keyword

variants: Check competition. Count the reviews on the top 3 Amazon results.
Check competition. Look at the star ratings on the top Amazon results.
Check competition. Write the review count on the best-selling version.
tasks.why: Review counts show how crowded or open a space is.

variants: Gut check. Write one sentence — does this idea seem doable?
Gut check. Write yes or no — does this idea still feel right?
Gut check. Jot your honest reaction to what you saw today.
tasks.why: Checking in with yourself keeps you honest.

variants: Find the gap. Read 5 one-star reviews of a top Amazon competitor.
Find the gap. Search your product on Amazon and read 3 bad reviews.
Find the gap. Open Amazon and write down 2 complaints buyers leave.
tasks.why: Bad reviews show exactly what customers wish existed.

variants: Spot the angle. Write one way your product could fix those complaints.
Spot the angle. Write one thing you'd do differently than competitors.
Spot the angle. Write one improvement buyers keep asking for.
tasks.why: A clear angle makes your product worth buying.

variants: Write your idea. One sentence: what would make your version better?
Write your idea. One sentence: how is your product different?
Write your idea. One sentence: what problem does your product solve?
tasks.why: A simple positioning statement guides every decision.

variants: Know your buyer. Write 3 words describing who would buy your product.
Know your buyer. Name the type of person most likely to buy this.
Know your buyer. Write age, hobby, and one problem of your ideal buyer.
tasks.why: Knowing your buyer makes marketing much easier later.

variants: Find them online. Search your product on TikTok and watch 3 videos.
Find them online. Search your product on Instagram and look at 5 posts.
Find them online. Google your product plus 'reddit' and read one thread.
tasks.why: Watching real buyers talk shows you what they care about.

variants: Write one phrase. Copy one thing a real buyer said online.
Write one phrase. Write the exact words a buyer used to describe the need.
Write one phrase. Note one complaint or wish you saw a buyer post.
tasks.why: Using buyer language in your copy converts better.

variants: Write a tagline. One short line that says what you sell.
Make a tagline. A single sentence that hooks new shoppers.
Craft a tagline. Sum up your store in under 10 words.
tasks.why: Tagline locks in brand voice early

variants: Find a supplier. Search your product on AliExpress right now.
Find a supplier. Open AliExpress and search your product idea.
Find a supplier. Search your product on Alibaba and look at 3 options.
tasks.why: Supplier cost determines whether you can make money.

variants: Do the math. Write: supplier price x3 — can you sell it for that?
Do the math. Write the cost from AliExpress and the Amazon selling price.
Do the math. Would you make at least $10 profit per sale? Write yes or no.
tasks.why: A rough margin check saves weeks of wasted work.

variants: Name your store. Write 5 two-word name ideas for your brand.
Name your store. Brainstorm 5 short names that fit your product.
Name your store. Write 5 names you'd actually want on a logo.
tasks.why: A name you love keeps you motivated to build.

variants: Check availability. Google your top 2 name ideas to see if they're taken.
Check availability. Search your top name on Instagram and see if it's free.
Check availability. Type your top name into Shopify signup to check it.
tasks.why: Checking early saves the pain of rebranding later.

variants: Pick one name. Write your favorite and say it out loud once.
Pick one name. Write the one you'd be proud to put on a package.
Pick one name. Choose the one that's easiest to remember.
tasks.why: Committing to a name lets you move forward.

variants: Weekly review. List 3 things you learned about your product this week.
Weekly review. Write what you know now that you didn't know Monday.
Weekly review. Write the most surprising thing you found this week.
tasks.why: Reviewing locks in learning and shows your progress.

variants: Confirm your niche. Write one sentence: what am I selling and to whom?
Confirm your niche. Write your product and your buyer in one sentence.
Confirm your niche. Write: I sell ___ to people who want ___.
tasks.why: A clear one-liner keeps every future decision focused.

variants: Set next goal. Write the one thing you want done by next Sunday.
Set next goal. Write what would feel like a win by end of next week.
Set next goal. Write one concrete action you'll finish before day 14.
tasks.why: A weekly target turns effort into momentum.

variants: Pick a color palette. Choose 3 brand colors that feel right.
Set your brand colors. Pick a primary, secondary, and accent tone.
Define your palette. Select 3 colors that match your store vibe.
tasks.why: Color palette gives brand consistency

variants: Note what works. Write 2 things that store does well.
Note what works. Write their headline and their main product description.
Note what works. Write the first thing you noticed on their homepage.
tasks.why: Spotting what works saves you from guessing.

variants: Steal one idea. Write one thing you'd copy from that store.
Steal one idea. Write one element you want to use in your own store.
Steal one idea. Write their best move you'll use when you launch.
tasks.why: Learning from success is smarter than starting from scratch.

variants: Pick your platform. Search 'Shopify vs Etsy vs Amazon' for 2 minutes.
Pick your platform. Read one Reddit post comparing Shopify and Etsy.
Pick your platform. Watch a 2-minute YouTube comparison of selling platforms.
tasks.why: Your platform choice shapes every step that follows.

variants: Weigh the pros. Write one pro and one con of Shopify for your situation.
Weigh the pros. Write why one platform fits your product better than another.
Weigh the pros. Write which platform your target buyer uses most.
tasks.why: Matching platform to product cuts wasted setup time.

variants: Choose one. Write which platform you'll start on and one reason why.
Choose one. Write your platform pick and what made you decide.
Choose one. Write the platform name and one thing you like about it.
tasks.why: Deciding now keeps you from getting stuck in research forever.

variants: Validate demand. Search your product on Google Trends right now.
Validate demand. Open Google Trends and type your product idea.
Validate demand. Check Google Trends — is your product trending up or down?
tasks.why: Trending up means buyers are growing, not shrinking.

variants: Check social proof. Search your product on TikTok and count the views on 3 videos.
Check social proof. Find a TikTok about your product and read the comments.
Check social proof. Search your product hashtag on Instagram and note the post count.
tasks.why: High views and engagement confirm real buyer interest.

variants: Write your verdict. One sentence: is this product worth pursuing?
Write your verdict. Write yes or no — and one reason for your answer.
Write your verdict. Would you bet $50 on this product? Write why.
tasks.why: A clear verdict keeps you from drifting for weeks.

variants: Write your IG bio. One line about what you sell and who it's for.
Update your Instagram bio. Keep it short and benefit-focused.
Set your IG bio. Say what you offer in two punchy lines.
tasks.why: IG bio drives first social impressions

variants: Compare options. Write the price and shipping time from 2 different suppliers.
Compare options. Note which supplier has the most reviews and best rating.
Compare options. Write the cost difference between your top 2 supplier options.
tasks.why: Comparing suppliers protects your margin and delivery time.

variants: Save your pick. Bookmark or screenshot your top supplier option.
Save your pick. Write the supplier name and link in a notes app.
Save your pick. Copy the supplier URL and save it somewhere you'll find it.
tasks.why: Saving a supplier link now prevents starting over later.

variants: Write your hook. Write one sentence that explains what you sell.
Write your hook. Finish this: 'I help people who want ___ by selling ___.'
Write your hook. Write what you sell and who you sell it to in 10 words.
tasks.why: A clear hook guides your whole brand message.

variants: Write 3 benefits. List 3 ways your product improves someone's life.
Write 3 benefits. Write 3 things a buyer gets from your product.
Write 3 benefits. Write 3 problems your product solves.
tasks.why: Benefits sell products — features alone don't.

variants: Pick your best. Circle the one benefit that's most compelling.
Pick your best. Write the one benefit you'd put on a billboard.
Pick your best. Write the benefit that would make you click 'buy.'
tasks.why: Your strongest benefit becomes your headline.

variants: Plan your brand look. Write 2 colors that fit your product's vibe.
Plan your brand look. Write 3 adjectives that describe your brand's feel.
Plan your brand look. Write whether your brand feels fun, clean, bold, or calm.
tasks.why: Brand feel guides every visual choice you'll make.

variants: Find inspiration. Search your niche on Pinterest and save 3 images.
Find inspiration. Open Instagram and save 3 posts that match your brand feel.
Find inspiration. Search your product type on Dribbble and note 1 design you like.
tasks.why: A mood board makes your store look intentional, not random.

variants: Write it down. Note your 2 colors and 3 adjectives in a notes file.
Write it down. Write your brand's vibe in one sentence you can refer back to.
Write it down. Write the look you're going for so you remember it.
tasks.why: Writing it down turns vague vision into a reference you'll use.

variants: Write a product description. Cover what it does and why it's great.
Describe your top product. Highlight the key benefit in 3 sentences.
Draft a product description. Focus on the problem it solves.
tasks.why: Product descriptions drive conversions

variants: Fix one gap. Write one thing you're still unsure about and search it for 2 minutes.
Fix one gap. Write the biggest question you still have and Google it.
Fix one gap. Write one thing that's fuzzy and look it up right now.
tasks.why: Small gaps compound into big roadblocks if left unaddressed.

variants: Commit to phase 2. Write: 'By day 30 I will have a working store.'
Commit to phase 2. Write one sentence about what launching feels like.
Commit to phase 2. Write the date you'll have your store live.
tasks.why: A commitment written down is more likely to happen.

variants: Open Shopify. Start a free trial at shopify.com right now.
Create your account. Go to your chosen platform and sign up today.
Start the trial. Open Shopify or Etsy and create your seller account.
tasks.why: An account is the first real step — everything else is planning.

variants: Set your store name. Enter your brand name in the store setup field.
Fill in basics. Type your store name and email in the setup wizard.
Name your store. Enter the name you picked in your Shopify account.
tasks.why: Filling in the basics gets you past the starting line.

variants: Pick a theme. Browse free Shopify themes for 60 seconds and choose one.
Pick a theme. Open the theme store and click the first one that fits your brand.
Pick a theme. Choose any free theme — you can change it later.
tasks.why: Any theme beats a blank store — pick and move on.

variants: Write your headline. Type a 6-word homepage headline in your store editor.
Write your headline. Open the homepage editor and write your top benefit.
Write your headline. Type the one sentence that explains what you sell.
tasks.why: A clear headline tells visitors why they should stay.

variants: Add your logo. Upload a simple text logo using Canva for free.
Add your logo. Open Canva, make a text logo with your brand name, download it.
Add your logo. Create a free logo at canva.com and upload it to your store.
tasks.why: A logo makes your store look like a real brand instantly.

variants: Set your colors. Apply your 2 brand colors in the theme settings.
Set your colors. Open theme settings and update the primary color to match your brand.
Set your colors. Change the theme accent color to one of your brand colors.
tasks.why: Consistent color makes your store look intentional.

variants: Write your brand story. Two paragraphs on why you started this.
Tell your brand story. Explain who you are and what drives you.
Draft your origin story. Share why you built this store in 3 sentences.
tasks.why: Brand story builds trust with buyers

variants: Write a description. Write 3 sentences about what your product does.
Write a description. Write the top 3 benefits of your product in the description box.
Write a description. Fill in the description with who it's for and what it does.
tasks.why: A clear description answers buyer questions before they ask.

variants: Set a price. Enter your selling price based on your margin math.
Set a price. Type a price that gives you at least $10 profit per sale.
Set a price. Enter your price — you can adjust it later after testing.
tasks.why: Having a price makes the product real and sellable.

variants: Get product images. Download 3 free product images from your AliExpress supplier.
Get product images. Save supplier images from your product listing to use temporarily.
Get product images. Find 2 free stock images of your product on Unsplash.
tasks.why: Images are the first thing buyers look at before reading anything.

variants: Upload images. Add your 3 images to the product listing in Shopify.
Upload images. Open your product in Shopify and add the images you saved.
Upload images. Attach your product images to the listing so buyers can see them.
tasks.why: A listing without photos gets ignored.

variants: Preview it. Click 'Preview' on your product and look at it like a buyer.
Preview it. Open the product preview and read it as if you're a first-time visitor.
Preview it. View your listing and write one thing you'd improve.
tasks.why: Seeing it live catches problems you'd miss while editing.

variants: Write your About page. Write 3 sentences about why you started this store.
Write your About page. Type why you made this product and who it's for.
Write your About page. Write one paragraph — your story in 3 simple sentences.
tasks.why: An About page builds trust with first-time buyers.

variants: Add a Contact page. Create a contact page with your email address.
Add a Contact page. Add a contact form to your Shopify store in Pages.
Add a Contact page. Set up a simple way for buyers to reach you.
tasks.why: A contact page makes buyers feel safe buying from you.

variants: Check navigation. Make sure Home, Products, and About appear in your menu.
Check navigation. Click every menu link in your store and make sure it works.
Check navigation. Add any missing pages to your store's main menu.
tasks.why: Broken navigation kills trust before buyers even see your product.

variants: Build a hashtag set. Write 15 tags that fit your niche.
Choose your hashtags. Pick 15 tags your target buyers follow.
Create a hashtag list. Find 15 relevant tags for your posts.
tasks.why: Hashtags expand organic reach on social

variants: Set up shipping. Go to Shopify Settings > Shipping and add one shipping rate.
Set up shipping. Set a flat shipping rate or free shipping for your store.
Set up shipping. Add one shipping zone and rate so orders can be fulfilled.
tasks.why: Shipping settings let your store actually process real orders.

variants: Test your store. Go to your store URL and click through it like a buyer.
Test your store. Open your store on your phone and see how it looks.
Test your store. Browse your store for 60 seconds and note one thing to fix.
tasks.why: Testing from the buyer's perspective reveals obvious problems fast.

variants: Connect a supplier. Install DSers or AutoDS on Shopify to link your AliExpress product.
Connect a supplier. Open Shopify App Store and install a dropshipping app.
Connect a supplier. Link your AliExpress product to your Shopify listing today.
tasks.why: Connecting a supplier means orders can be fulfilled automatically.

variants: Check the sync. Confirm your product is linked and inventory shows in Shopify.
Check the sync. Open your product and confirm it's connected to the supplier.
Check the sync. Make sure your product listing shows as 'active' in Shopify.
tasks.why: A broken supplier link means orders go unfulfilled — check it now.

variants: Weekly check-in. Write what your store has that it didn't have 7 days ago.
Weekly check-in. List 3 real things you built in your store this week.
Weekly check-in. Write the biggest thing you finished this week.
tasks.why: Seeing your progress keeps you motivated to keep going.

variants: Improve your title. Rewrite your product title to include who it's for.
Improve your title. Add a benefit or use case to your product title.
Improve your title. Rewrite your title to be more specific than it is now.
tasks.why: A specific title gets found in search and clicked more.

variants: Improve your description. Add one more benefit to your product description.
Improve your description. Rewrite one sentence to sound more like a real person wrote it.
Improve your description. Add a line that says who this product is perfect for.
tasks.why: Better copy turns browsers into buyers.

variants: Add a call to action. Write 'Order yours today' somewhere on the product page.
Add a call to action. Make sure your Buy button has clear text like 'Add to Cart.'
Add a call to action. Add one line that tells the buyer what to do next.
tasks.why: A clear call to action increases the chance someone buys.

variants: Write 3 ad headlines. Short lines that could stop a scroll.
Draft ad headlines. Three punchy one-liners that sell your product.
Make 3 ad headlines. Each one highlights a different benefit.
tasks.why: Ad headlines are reusable across paid channels

variants: Fill in the bio. Write a 1-sentence bio that says what you sell and who it's for.
Fill in the bio. Add your store link and a 10-word description to your Instagram bio.
Fill in the bio. Write a bio that answers: what do you sell and why does it matter?
tasks.why: A clear bio turns profile visitors into store visitors.

variants: Post one image. Share one product image with a one-line caption.
Post one image. Upload your best product photo and write a simple caption.
Post one image. Share a product photo with a caption describing one benefit.
tasks.why: One post makes you an active brand, not just a profile.

variants: Study a TikTok. Find a TikTok video about your product type with 10k+ views.
Study a TikTok. Search your product on TikTok and watch the most popular video.
Study a TikTok. Find the most viewed TikTok about your product and watch it twice.
tasks.why: High-view videos show you a proven content formula to copy.

variants: Write the script. Write 3 sentences you'd say in a video about your product.
Write the script. Write one hook sentence and two benefit lines for a product video.
Write the script. Write what you'd say in the first 5 seconds of a TikTok.
tasks.why: A script stops you from going blank when you hit record.

variants: Plan your post. Write 1 TikTok idea for your product you could film today.
Plan your post. Write the concept for your first product video in one sentence.
Plan your post. Write what you'd film if you had to post a video right now.
tasks.why: Planning a video idea removes the excuse not to make one.

variants: Order a sample. Order one unit from your supplier to see the real product.
Order a sample. Go to AliExpress and order 1 sample of your product.
Order a sample. Place a test order from your top supplier right now.
tasks.why: Seeing the real product lets you sell it honestly and confidently.

variants: Note the cost. Write exactly what it cost to order the sample including shipping.
Note the cost. Record the sample total so you can factor it into your margin.
Note the cost. Write the sample price and shipping time you were shown.
tasks.why: Real costs are always slightly different from what you expect.

variants: Set an expectation. Write when the sample will arrive and add it to your calendar.
Set an expectation. Note the delivery date and plan to film content when it arrives.
Set an expectation. Write the estimated arrival date somewhere you'll see it.
tasks.why: Tracking the arrival date keeps your launch timeline on track.

variants: Write your landing page hero. One headline and one subhead.
Draft your hero section. Bold headline plus a short supporting line.
Create your hero copy. A strong opener for your homepage.
tasks.why: Hero section is the first thing visitors see

variants: Use hashtags. Add 5 relevant hashtags to your post right now.
Use hashtags. Research 3 hashtags your ideal buyer follows and use them.
Use hashtags. Add hashtags to your latest post that your target buyer uses.
tasks.why: Hashtags are free discovery — they put your post in front of strangers.

variants: Engage. Like and comment on 3 posts in your niche today.
Engage. Comment something genuine on 2 posts from accounts in your space.
Engage. Follow 5 accounts in your niche and leave one real comment.
tasks.why: Engaging with your niche community gets you noticed organically.

variants: Write a FAQ. Write 3 questions a buyer might ask about your product.
Write a FAQ. Write the top 3 questions people have before buying your product.
Write a FAQ. Think of 3 things that might stop someone from buying — write them.
tasks.why: Answering questions upfront removes the last reason not to buy.

variants: Answer the FAQ. Write one-sentence answers to each of your 3 questions.
Answer the FAQ. Write a short answer to each buyer question you listed.
Answer the FAQ. Write a plain-English answer to your top buyer objection.
tasks.why: Clear answers build trust that makes people click buy.

variants: Add to your store. Paste the FAQ into your product description or a new page.
Add to your store. Copy the FAQ into your product page in Shopify.
Add to your store. Add the Q&A to the bottom of your product description.
tasks.why: FAQs on the product page reduce abandoned carts.

variants: Write a return policy. Write 2 sentences on what you'll do if a buyer is unhappy.
Write a return policy. Write your refund rule in plain English in 2 sentences.
Write a return policy. Write what a buyer can do if the product arrives broken.
tasks.why: A return policy makes buyers feel safe enough to click buy.

variants: Add a policy page. Go to Shopify > Pages and create a Refund Policy page.
Add a policy page. Paste your return policy into a new page in your Shopify store.
Add a policy page. Create a policy page in your store and add it to the footer.
tasks.why: Policy pages are required for trust — and sometimes legally.

variants: Check the footer. Make sure your policy page link appears in the store footer.
Check the footer. Click your footer links and confirm the policy page loads.
Check the footer. Add your return policy and contact links to the footer.
tasks.why: Buyers look in the footer for policies — make it easy to find.

variants: Build a content calendar. Plan 7 posts for the next week.
Map out your content. Schedule one post per day for 7 days.
Create a posting plan. Write topics for the next 7 days.
tasks.why: Content calendar keeps social consistent

variants: Fix one thing. Find one broken or ugly part of your store and fix it now.
Fix one thing. Pick the most embarrassing page in your store and improve it.
Fix one thing. Write what looks off and change it in the editor right now.
tasks.why: Fixing one thing a day keeps your store improving without overwhelm.

variants: Share your store. Send your store URL to one person you trust and ask for feedback.
Share your store. Text your store link to one friend and ask 'Would you buy this?'
Share your store. Show your store to one person today and write their reaction.
tasks.why: Real feedback from a real person reveals what data can't.

variants: Month one review. Write 5 things you've built in the last 30 days.
Month one review. List your store name, product, platform, supplier, and social account.
Month one review. Write what your store looked like on day 1 vs today.
tasks.why: Reviewing month one shows how far you've come and what's left.

variants: Spot your gap. Write the one thing your store is still missing.
Spot your gap. Write the #1 reason someone might not buy from you right now.
Spot your gap. Write what you'd fix first if you had 30 more minutes today.
tasks.why: Naming the gap is the first step to closing it.

variants: Set month 2 goal. Write one thing you want to achieve by day 60.
Set month 2 goal. Write your first sale goal — date and amount.
Set month 2 goal. Write: 'By day 60 I will have ___.' Fill in the blank.
tasks.why: A written goal for month 2 keeps momentum alive.

variants: Film your first TikTok. Record a 15-second video showing your product.
Film your first TikTok. Hold your product and film a 10-second demo.
Film your first TikTok. Record yourself saying one benefit of your product.
tasks.why: Your first video doesn't need to be perfect — it needs to exist.

variants: Add a caption. Write a 1-sentence caption and 3 hashtags for your video.
Add a caption. Write the first line of your TikTok caption — make it a question.
Add a caption. Write a caption that tells viewers exactly what you're showing.
tasks.why: A clear caption boosts reach and tells the algorithm what your video is about.

variants: Post it. Upload the video to TikTok or Instagram Reels right now.
Post it. Hit publish on your video — done is better than perfect.
Post it. Upload your video now and don't overthink it.
tasks.why: Every video teaches you something — but only after you post it.

variants: Write a welcome email. Short message for new subscribers.
Draft your welcome email. Introduce yourself and set expectations.
Create a welcome email. Tell new sign-ups what they'll get.
tasks.why: Welcome emails set the tone for the customer relationship

variants: Plan video 2. Write a second TikTok idea based on what you saw in your niche.
Plan video 2. Write a concept for your next video — different angle from the first.
Plan video 2. Write one new TikTok idea you could film today.
tasks.why: A second video idea keeps you from stopping after one.

variants: Film or schedule. Either film video 2 now or write when you will.
Film or schedule. Record your second video or write the exact day you'll film it.
Film or schedule. Commit to a filming date for your next video in writing.
tasks.why: Scheduling removes the 'I'll do it later' trap.

variants: Research ads. Search 'TikTok ads for beginners' and read for 2 minutes.
Research ads. Watch a 2-minute YouTube video on running a small TikTok ad.
Research ads. Search 'Facebook ads ecommerce beginner' and read one article.
tasks.why: Understanding ads before you run them saves wasted money.

variants: Write your ad hook. Write a 1-sentence opening line for a product ad.
Write your ad hook. Write the first thing you'd say to stop someone from scrolling.
Write your ad hook. Write a question that would make your buyer stop and watch.
tasks.why: The hook is 80% of whether an ad works.

variants: Set a budget. Write the dollar amount you're willing to test ads with.
Set a budget. Decide your first ad budget — write a number you can afford to lose.
Set a budget. Write: I'll spend $__ on my first ad test.
tasks.why: Setting a limit upfront protects you from overspending.

variants: Write ad copy. Write a 3-sentence ad: hook, benefit, call to action.
Write ad copy. Write one ad: first line grabs attention, second line explains, third says buy.
Write ad copy. Write a short ad script — problem, solution, link.
tasks.why: Written ad copy is the core of every paid ad you'll ever run.

variants: Choose the visual. Pick one image or video clip to use in your first ad.
Choose the visual. Decide if your first ad will use a photo or short video.
Choose the visual. Pick your best product image to use as the ad creative.
tasks.why: The visual paired with the hook determines your click-through rate.

variants: Draft the ad. Write the headline, body text, and call to action in one place.
Draft the ad. Combine your hook, benefit, and CTA into one complete ad draft.
Draft the ad. Write your full ad copy in a notes file so it's ready to paste.
tasks.why: Having the ad drafted means you can launch it the moment you're ready.

variants: Write a launch post. Announce your store opening on social.
Draft your launch announcement. One post that builds excitement.
Create a launch post. Share your store is open and ready.
tasks.why: Launch post drives initial traffic

variants: Enter billing. Add your payment method to the ad account.
Enter billing. Connect a card to your ad account so you're ready to spend.
Enter billing. Set up billing in your ad platform so it won't block your first campaign.
tasks.why: Billing setup is the boring step most people delay — do it now.

variants: Write what's next. Write the one thing you need to do before you run your first ad.
Write what's next. List the 2 things between you and launching your first ad.
Write what's next. Write what's missing before your first ad goes live.
tasks.why: Writing the next step makes it easier to start tomorrow.

variants: Post video 3. Film and post your third piece of content today.
Post video 3. Record and upload your third TikTok or Reel.
Post video 3. Create and post your third piece of social content.
tasks.why: Three posts builds a real content library, not just a one-off.

variants: Try a new format. Make this video different — unboxing, review, or tutorial.
Try a new format. Switch up your content type from the last 2 posts.
Try a new format. Film a 'how to use it' video instead of a product showcase.
tasks.why: Testing formats tells you which style your audience responds to most.

variants: Note the difference. Write how this video felt different to make vs the last one.
Note the difference. Write which format felt more natural to film.
Note the difference. Write which video idea was easiest to execute.
tasks.why: The easiest format to make is often the one you'll stick with.

variants: Check your sample. If it arrived, take it out and film a reaction video.
Check your sample. Open the package and write 3 things about the real product.
Check your sample. Hold the real product and write: does it match the listing photos?
tasks.why: Seeing the real product tells you if your supplier is worth keeping.

variants: Quality check. Rate the product quality 1-10 and write one reason.
Quality check. Write what a buyer would say when they open this product.
Quality check. Write one thing you love and one thing you'd improve about the product.
tasks.why: Selling a quality product you'd use yourself is the only sustainable approach.

variants: Decide. Write: keep this supplier or find a better one — and why.
Decide. Write yes or no — is this product good enough to sell?

Check giveaway results. Write how many entries the giveaway received.
Check giveaway results. Open the giveaway post and count comments and follows gained.
tasks.why: Giveaway data tells you whether the tactic is worth repeating.

variants: Pick a winner. Randomly select a winner from the comments and DM them.
Pick a winner. Use a random name picker and message the winner today.
Pick a winner. Choose a winner publicly in a follow-up post or story.
tasks.why: Announcing a winner builds trust and encourages future engagement.

variants: Announce it. Post a short 'winner announced' update to keep the momentum going.
Announce it. Share the winner's name on your story or in a new post.
Announce it. Post 'Congrats to our winner!' and tag them — it drives comments.
tasks.why: Public winner announcements prove the giveaway was real and builds goodwill.

variants: Improve your SEO. Add a keyword to your product title that buyers search for.
Improve your SEO. Search your product on Google and add the top search term to your title.
Improve your SEO. Rewrite your product title to include how buyers describe it in searches.
tasks.why: SEO is free traffic — one better title can bring in buyers forever.

variants: Add a meta description. Write a 1-sentence meta description for your product page.
Add a meta description. Fill in the SEO section of your Shopify product with a keyword-rich sentence.
Add a meta description. Edit the SEO preview in Shopify and write a clear meta description.
tasks.why: A meta description makes your page clickable when people find it in Google.

variants: Check it. Google your store name and see if it appears in search results.
Check it. Search your product on Google and see where your store ranks.
Check it. Google your brand name and write whether your store shows up.
tasks.why: Checking visibility tells you whether your SEO work is having any effect.

variants: Write an influencer pitch DM. Short ask to partner on a post.
Draft an influencer outreach. Introduce your brand and propose a collab.
Create an influencer DM. Keep it friendly and under 4 sentences.
tasks.why: Influencer pitches expand reach quickly

variants: Boost it. Put $10 behind this video as a paid promotion.
Boost it. Promote video 9 with a $10 boost to your target audience.
Boost it. Spend $10 amplifying this video to people who match your buyer profile.
tasks.why: Boosting a video that already looks authentic beats any cold ad creative.

variants: Track conversions. Write how many store visits you get in the next 24 hours.
Track conversions. Note your store traffic in Shopify analytics after boosting.
Track conversions. Open Shopify analytics tomorrow and write the sessions from today.
tasks.why: Tracking visits after a boost tells you if the content is driving real interest.

variants: Three-quarter review. Write 3 things that are working and 1 thing that isn't.
Three-quarter review. List your content, ads, email, and social — what's performing?
Three-quarter review. Write: what's brought the most traffic so far?
tasks.why: A structured review at day 75 tells you where to focus your final sprint.

variants: Double down on one channel. Write which channel — TikTok, ads, or email — to push hardest.
Double down on one channel. Pick your best traffic source and write how to do more of it.
Double down on one channel. Write the one thing you'll do every day for the last 15 days.
tasks.why: Focused effort in one channel beats scattered effort in three.

variants: Commit to the final push. Write: 'My goal by day 90 is ___.' Fill it in.
Commit to the final push. Write your first-sale target in plain language.
Commit to the final push. Write what you'll do this week that you haven't tried yet.
tasks.why: The last 15 days require intention — write your target before they start.

variants: Post daily. Film and post your 10th piece of content today.
Post daily. Create your 10th TikTok or Reel — ten posts is a real library.
Post daily. Make your 10th video — try a 'myth vs. fact' format about your product.
tasks.why: Ten posts is the point where your account starts to look established.

variants: Run a bigger ad. Raise your best campaign to $15/day for the final push.
Run a bigger ad. Increase your top ad to $15/day — more spend, more data.
Run a bigger ad. Bump your winning ad budget to $15 daily for the next 2 weeks.
tasks.why: The last sprint deserves more fuel — spend more on what's already working.

variants: Write your sales goal. Write: 'I want ___ sales by day 90.' Put a number on it.
Write your sales goal. Write the exact number of orders you want in 14 days.
Write your sales goal. Write your revenue target for the final 2 weeks.
tasks.why: A number in writing is more motivating than a vague hope.

variants: Design a static ad creative. Pick a template with your product photo.
Create a static ad image. Combine your logo, photo, and headline.
Build an ad creative. A simple image with your product and tagline.
tasks.why: Static ad creatives are needed for paid social campaigns

variants: Write retargeting copy. Write a 2-sentence ad: 'You saw this. Here's 10% off to try it.'
Write retargeting copy. Write a retargeting ad that mentions they visited but didn't buy.
Write retargeting copy. Write a short ad that includes your discount code as the hook.
tasks.why: Retargeting copy that acknowledges the visitor's behavior converts best.

variants: Launch it. Set your retargeting ad live at $5/day.
Launch it. Publish your retargeting campaign and set a $5 daily budget.
Launch it. Start the retargeting ad today — it's your lowest-cost path to a sale.
tasks.why: Retargeting is the fastest way to convert people who already showed interest.

variants: Post video 11. Film your 11th video — try a 'top 3 reasons to buy' format.
Post video 11. Make your 11th TikTok or Reel listing 3 reasons your product is worth it.
Post video 11. Record a '3 reasons' video and post it today.
tasks.why: List-format videos consistently outperform single-benefit videos.

variants: Include your offer. Say your discount code out loud in the video.
Include your offer. Add a text overlay showing your code and store link.
Include your offer. End the video with 'Use code ___ at [store link] in bio.'
tasks.why: A spoken offer in video outperforms a text offer in a caption.

variants: Pin it. Pin this video to the top of your TikTok or Instagram profile.
Pin it. Set this as your pinned post so every profile visitor sees it first.
Pin it. Make this your profile's first impression by pinning it now.
tasks.why: A pinned post is a permanent first impression for every new profile visitor.

variants: Run a flash sale. Create a 24-hour 20% off code in Shopify right now.
Run a flash sale. Set up a one-day sale code and post about it immediately.
Run a flash sale. Create a code that expires tomorrow and tell your followers.
tasks.why: A flash sale creates urgency that turns followers into buyers.

variants: Post the flash sale. Share it on TikTok, Instagram, and Stories right now.
Post the flash sale. Announce the 24-hour sale on every platform you're on.
Post the flash sale. Go live with the sale across all your social channels.
tasks.why: Multi-channel posting maximizes how many people see the urgency.

variants: Set a reminder. Schedule a 'last chance' post for tonight or tomorrow morning.
Set a reminder. Write a follow-up post for 4 hours before the sale ends.
Set a reminder. Plan a 'hours left' story or post to run before the sale closes.
tasks.why: A reminder post near the deadline captures people who need one more nudge.

variants: Make a launch checklist. List every step before going live.
Write a pre-launch checklist. Cover store, social, email, and ads.
Create a go-live checklist. Check off every item before launch day.
tasks.why: Launch checklists prevent costly missed steps

variants: Analyze the gap. If no sales: was it traffic or conversion? Write your best guess.
Analyze the gap. Write where buyers dropped off — at the ad, the store, or checkout.
Analyze the gap. Write the one change that might have turned a visitor into a buyer.
tasks.why: Finding the gap is how you close it before the 90 days end.

variants: Make one fix. Based on your analysis, change one thing in your store or ad right now.
Make one fix. Fix the single most likely reason someone didn't complete a purchase.
Make one fix. Apply one change from your analysis immediately.
tasks.why: One fix per day is how stores go from zero to one sale.

variants: Post video 12. Make your 12th video — use the structure of your top-viewed post.
Post video 12. Film and post your 12th piece of content today.
Post video 12. Record your 12th video — recreate your best post with fresh content.
tasks.why: Twelve posts gives the algorithm enough to understand what you make.

variants: Use a trending topic. Find a trend in your niche and make a video responding to it.
Use a trending topic. Search your niche hashtag and find a trending conversation to join.
Use a trending topic. Make a video that references something trending in your product space.
tasks.why: Trend-reactive content gets boosted by the algorithm and search.

variants: Measure follower growth. Write how many followers you have vs. 30 days ago.
Measure follower growth. Check your follower count and write the growth since day 46.
Measure follower growth. Write your current follower count and set a 10-day target.
tasks.why: Follower growth shows whether your content strategy is working at all.

variants: Reach out to 5 influencers. Send your collab pitch to 5 new creators today.
Reach out to 5 influencers. Find 5 micro-influencers and send your DM to each one.
Reach out to 5 influencers. DM 5 creators in your niche with your sample offer today.
tasks.why: Volume is the strategy — most DMs get ignored, so send more.

variants: Personalize one pitch. Rewrite one DM to reference something specific about that creator.
Personalize one pitch. Mention one video of theirs in your pitch to show you actually watched.
Personalize one pitch. Write a custom pitch for your most promising influencer lead.
tasks.why: One personalized message converts better than ten generic ones.

variants: Follow up on old DMs. Reply to any influencer who hasn't responded in 5+ days.
Follow up on old DMs. Send a short follow-up to your top 2 influencer leads.
Follow up on old DMs. Check your sent DMs and follow up on any that got no reply.
tasks.why: A polite follow-up often converts a non-reply into a yes.

variants: Write your pricing page. Show tiers or product bundles clearly.
Draft pricing page copy. Make each option easy to compare.
Create a pricing section. List what buyers get at each price point.
tasks.why: Pricing pages reduce buying friction

variants: Improve your buy button. Change your 'Add to Cart' text to something more specific.
Improve your buy button. Try 'Get Yours Today' or 'Claim Your Discount' as the button text.
Improve your buy button. Write a more action-oriented button label and update it in Shopify.
tasks.why: Button text changes have a direct, measurable effect on conversion rate.

variants: A/B test your price. Try raising your price by $5 for the next 7 days.
A/B test your price. Write down your current price, then raise it by $5 and observe.
A/B test your price. Increase your price slightly — buyers often trust higher prices more.
tasks.why: A higher price sometimes converts better because it signals quality.

variants: Post video 13. Film and post your 13th piece of content — try a customer FAQ format.
Post video 13. Make your 13th TikTok answering the most common question about your product.
Post video 13. Record a FAQ video and post it today.
tasks.why: FAQ videos build trust and attract buyers who are almost ready to buy.

variants: Mention your discount. Say your code and store link clearly at the end of the video.
Mention your discount. Add your discount code as a text overlay in the final seconds.
Mention your discount. End with a clear CTA: store link and discount code.
tasks.why: Every video is an opportunity to drive a sale — don't waste the ending.

variants: Check sessions. Open Shopify analytics and write how many sessions you had today.
Check sessions. Write your store's daily visitor count from the Shopify dashboard.
Check sessions. Look at Shopify analytics and write sessions vs. 2 weeks ago.
tasks.why: Daily session tracking shows you if your content and ads are sending traffic.

variants: Final ad push. Create one new ad using your absolute best video and copy.
Final ad push. Launch a new campaign with your strongest creative at $15/day.
Final ad push. Run your best ad with your best headline and image — max budget.
tasks.why: The last 5 days need your strongest effort, not your average one.

variants: Target a warm audience. Set the ad to target people who visited your store in the last 30 days.
Target a warm audience. Run the ad to your retargeting list — these people already know you.
Target a warm audience. Set the audience to website visitors plus lookalike buyers.
tasks.why: Warm audiences convert at a fraction of the cost of cold ones.

variants: Set a check-in. Write when you'll check this ad's performance — tomorrow, same time.
Set a check-in. Write the time tomorrow you'll review this ad's data.
Set a check-in. Put a reminder to check the ad results at the same time tomorrow.
tasks.why: A scheduled check-in keeps you from either ignoring or obsessing over the data.

variants: Write a testimonial request email. Ask happy buyers for a review.
Draft a review request. Short email asking customers for feedback.
Create a testimonial ask. A friendly note to satisfied shoppers.
tasks.why: Testimonials are social proof that drives conversions

variants: Offer a final sale. Post a 'last chance' sale with an expiry in 48 hours.
Offer a final sale. Create a 48-hour sale code and announce it on all your channels.
Offer a final sale. Run your last sale of the 90 days — make it your best offer.
tasks.why: A final sale creates the urgency that pushes uncommitted buyers to act.

variants: Message your list. Send a sale email to anyone who signed up or bought before.
Message your list. Email your subscribers with the final sale code today.
Message your list. Send a short sale email: code, offer, expiry date.
tasks.why: Your email list is the most direct path to a quick sale.

variants: Check results. Open Shopify and write your session count and any orders.
Check results. Look at your dashboard — sessions, add-to-carts, and purchases.
Check results. Write what happened in your store in the last 24 hours.
tasks.why: Three days from the end, your data shows exactly what's working.

variants: Make one last fix. Find the most broken thing in your funnel and fix it today.
Make one last fix. Pick the #1 barrier to purchase and remove it right now.
Make one last fix. Write what you'd fix if you had one hour — then fix it.
tasks.why: One last fix in the final days can be the difference between zero and one sale.

variants: Remind followers. Post a 'last chance' story or video about your current offer.
Remind followers. Post a reminder that your sale ends soon.
Remind followers. Share a last-call message across your social accounts.
tasks.why: Reminder posts catch everyone who saw the sale but forgot to act.

variants: Do a full audit. Open every page of your store and write one thing to improve on each.
Do a full audit. Check homepage, product page, checkout, and policies for issues.
Do a full audit. Click through your store like a first-time buyer and list what's off.
tasks.why: A fresh audit two days before the end catches the things you've gone blind to.

variants: Fix the biggest issue. Change the most embarrassing thing you found in the audit.
Fix the biggest issue. Fix the one issue that's most likely costing you a sale.
Fix the biggest issue. Edit the page or element that would most hurt a buyer's trust.
tasks.why: Fixing the worst issue first gives you the most return on the last minutes.

variants: Post one more. Film and post your 15th piece of content — make it your best.
Post one more. Record your 15th video and make it the most direct sell yet.
Post one more. Post your 15th piece of content — speak straight to a buyer.
tasks.why: Fifteen posts is a real content library that keeps working after day 90.

variants: Write a Q&A section. Add answers to 5 product-specific questions.
Draft product Q&As. Cover fit, use, and care in 5 short answers.
Create a product Q&A. Answer the questions buyers ask before buying.
tasks.why: Product Q&As reduce hesitation and returns

variants: Last influencer push. Send 3 more DMs to creators you haven't contacted yet.
Last influencer push. Follow up with every influencer you messaged who didn't reply.
Last influencer push. Send your best pitch to 3 new creators today.
tasks.why: Even one influencer post this close to the end can drive your first sale.

variants: Write tomorrow's plan. Write what you'll do on day 90 to push for a sale.
Write tomorrow's plan. Write the 3 things you'll do on the last day.
Write tomorrow's plan. Write your final-day action plan right now.
tasks.why: Planning the last day the night before means you start with intention, not panic.

variants: Write a 90-day recap post. Share what you built and what's next.
Draft a milestone post. Celebrate 90 days of building your store.
Create a progress update. One post summing up your store journey.
tasks.why: Day 90 milestone builds community and momentum

variants: Celebrate the win. Write one thing you're genuinely proud of from this journey.
Celebrate the win. Write the hardest thing you pushed through to get here.
Celebrate the win. Write one thing you did that you didn't think you could do.
tasks.why: Acknowledging effort is what makes you want to keep going past day 90.

variants: Set day 91. Write one thing you'll do tomorrow to keep the store growing.
Set day 91. Write your next goal now — don't let momentum stop at day 90.
Set day 91. Write: 'Tomorrow I will ___.' The work doesn't stop here.
tasks.why: Day 91 is where most people quit — writing it down means you won't.
